ksa_text: Knowledge of AI security threats and adversarial ML techniques, including data poisoning, model extraction, evasion attacks, prompt injection, and the defensive measures and robustness testing approaches applicable to each.

nice.top_element_label: Knowledge of Extensible Markup Language (XML) schemas

nist_ai_rmf.top_element_label: Policies, processes, procedures, and practices across the organization related to the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks are in place, transparent, and implemented effectively.

top_anchors.element_label: Knowledge of Extensible Markup Language (XML) schemas

pass_2_evaluation.rationale: Edge case — included on balance: knowledge contains 2 RSK-relevant signal(s) ("prompt injection", "robustness.{0,15} ?:test|assess") within AI & ML Infrastructure (domain affinity 0.72). STRM composite is moderate (0.3966). A practicing Bias Auditor would encounter this competency regularly.

pass_4_adversarial_external.challenge: External check: no matches in RSK job postings or cert curricula (CRISC, FRM, NIST AI RMF, CISM). Would a working Bias Auditor self-report this as required?
pass_4_adversarial_external.evidence: Challenge answered: the competency is implicit in Bias Auditor work (domain affinity 0.72, 2 RSK signal(s)). Framework vocabulary matching systematically underrepresents risk management and governance KSAs.
